item.title: Wallabies and All Blacks face similar dilemma when players head overseas
item.description: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.

item.title: Developers with state-tied projects donate to Queensland politicians after ban lifted
item.description: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

item.title: Collingwood sobering-up centre to close less than four years after public intoxication reforms
item.description: A 20-bed facility in the Melbourne suburb of Collingwood will close in 2027 after operating for just a few years following legal reforms.

item.title: Whyalla's steel and Port Pirie's lead are facing a make-or-break six months
item.description: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: A new front emerges, plus the fallout from Trump-Xi and whispers of renewed strikes
item.description: The UAE shifts from target to combatant amid reports the US and Israel are preparing to renew strikes on Iran, writes Matthew Doran.

item.title: Elk sighting in SA's south-east raises questions about deer farm escapes
item.description: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

item.title: Central Australian Aboriginal ranger camp celebrates importance of women in protecting country
item.description: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: New South Wales animal rescuers call for change amid a growing stray cat 'crisis'
item.description: Animal shelters across New South Wales sound the alarm on a worsening stray cat crisis.

item.title: No Walsh for Queensland as Blues coach accidentally lists former Maroon
item.description: Billy Slater leaves Reece Walsh out of his squad for State of Origin I, while coach Laurie Daley accidentally announces the name of an ex-Queensland player.

item.title: 1.2 million Queensland homes unprepared as smoke alarm deadline nears
item.description: A national company estimates many Queensland homes are unprepared as the deadline to install compliant smoke alarms nears.

item.title: Fashion designer Rochelle Gregory uses film to untangle her mental health battle
item.description: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

item.title: Taiwanese president says island will not be sacrificed after Trump-Xi meeting
item.description: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

item.title: Tasmanian Anglican Church to consider 'far-reaching' options to fund increasing redress obligations
item.description: Tasmania's Anglican Church says what it expects to pay in compensation to victim-survivors of child sexual abuse has more than doubled in the past three years.

item.title: Steven Miles squeaks a win in Stafford by-election, despite drop in Labor vote
item.description: Labor candidate Luke Richmond will become the next member for Stafford, but the party's vote dropped several points.

item.title: Court mention for 33-year-old charged with murder over Gooburrum death
item.description: Yutthawichan Phetrchaisak is remanded in custody after being charged with the murder of a 42-year-old man north of Bundaberg.

item.title: Live frog found in bag of lettuce in remote Western Australian town
item.description: A Kiwi couple in a remote port town on WA's south coast have shared an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.

item.title: Hundreds of holidaymakers in limbo as travel company AVG Travels cancels trips
item.description: Australian travellers say they have been dumped days and weeks before their overseas holidays after a tour company cancelled their trips.

item.title: WA mouse plague pushes regulator to approve stronger bait
item.description: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

item.title: Corbie Walpole, 25, appeals jail term for drunkenly setting friend on fire
item.description: Corbie Walpole's legal team has argued the sentencing judge failed to properly consider psychiatric evidence about her mental health at the time of the offending.

item.title: Federal treasurer orders more Chinese-linked investors to offload shares in Australian rare earths mine
item.description: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.

item.title: Man pleads not guilty to murder but guilty to manslaughter in park amputation trial
item.description: The trial of a man who allegedly performed an amputation on a 66-year-old, causing his death, is likely to focus on the accused's "state of mind" and intention, a court hears.

item.title: Nicola Spurrier shares road safety message after she was hit by car while cycling
item.description: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

item.title: State of Origin: Why did Billy Slater leave Reece Walsh out of his Game I Queensland Maroons team?
item.description: Reece Walsh's generational run to end 2025 was almost without peer, but he has rejoined the ranks of the mortals to start this year.

item.title: Make the most of cheap and versatile tinned beans with these recipes
item.description: From vegetarian chilli to hearty soup, these easy recipes might convince you to add beans to your regular dinner rotation.

item.title: Man jailed for assaulting off-duty police officer in Newtown has sentence reduced
item.description: Koby Weaver, 21, was sentenced last month to two years' jail, after he ran into a brawl between two of his friends and two off-duty officers.

item.title: Treasurer says 'death tax' claims are a scare campaign, 'pure and simple'
item.description: The Coalition seizes on the federal government's broken tax promises, claiming a "death tax" has been sneaked into changes to trusts passed on through inheritance. The treasurer says that is a scare campaign.

item.title: Trial begins, pitting pianist Jayson Gillham against Melbourne Symphony Orchestra over his Gaza comments
item.description: Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra in the Federal Court over political discrimination after comments he made about Gaza on stage during a 2024 performance.

item.title: Anglo American sells central Queensland coal mines to UK company
item.description: Anglo American says it has sold its five steelmaking coal mines in central Queensland to a UK mining company Dhilmar Ltd for up to $5.43 billion.

item.title: Cull of 20,000 native birds at Newcastle Waters cattle station will not go ahead after parent company intervened
item.description: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.

item.title: Australia's smelters are asking for more taxpayer funding to compete with China on critical minerals like antimony
item.description: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

item.title: Government blocks private colleges from offering new courses as part of student visa crackdown
item.description: Australia suspends private colleges from applying to offer new courses to international students as part of a crackdown on abuse in the student visa system.

item.title: Innisfail man accused of paying to watch live videos of overseas child sex abuse
item.description: Police allege the 38-year-old from the state's far north took part in more than 40 live shows on an online platform. WARNING: This story contains details of child sexual abuse.

item.title: Scott Pendlebury not looking too far into the future as AFL/VFL games record looms
item.description: Collingwood champion Pendlebury will break the AFL games record against West Coast but hasn't pondered his future beyond then.

item.title: Free public transport, extended timetables among solutions to NSW live music scene, inquiry told
item.description: An inquiry into the state of live music in NSW hears from industry stakeholders looking to service younger audiences sprawled across Greater Sydney.

item.title: Israeli forces intercept Gaza flotilla with Australians on board, organisers say
item.description: The organisers of a flotilla of ships trying to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza say Israel's navy is intercepting the group off the coast of Cyprus.

item.title: How WA's approach to shark mitigation has evolved over decades after another fatal attack
item.description: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.

item.title: Rajesh Jiwan, 56, charged with murder after woman's body found in Hobart home
item.description: A 56-year-old man has been charged with murder after the body of a woman was found in a Hobart residence over the weekend.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Clare Wright wins top prize at NSW Literary Awards for 'powerful' history book Näku Dhäruk The Bark Petitions
item.description: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: From retirement to record breaker, Romelda Aiken-George keeps reinventing herself
item.description: Romelda Aiken-George is supposed to be retired. Instead, the 37-year-old goal shooter is breaking all kinds of records and reinventing herself in her 19th season. After her 255th national league game on the weekend, she reveals the secret to her longevity.

item.title: Should we be worried about the latest Ebola outbreak?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Trial begins as WA police assistant commissioner Gailene Hamilton accused of stalking subordinate officer
item.description: A WA police assistant commissioner faces trial accused of stalking a female subordinate officer and using a restricted computer system to track her movements.

item.title: Man charged with murder after woman and two young children found dead in south-west Sydney home
item.description: A man, 47, has been charged after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police allege is a domestic violence-related incident.

item.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold
item.description: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sánchez's government could suffer an electoral wipe-out in a nationwide vote.

item.title: Iran International journalist Pouria Zaratifoukolaei stabbed in London at behest of Iran, court told
item.description: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

item.title: Two Americans arrested over man breaking into enclosure of Punch the monkey
item.description: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

item.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman
item.description: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

item.title: ABC Timelines quiz: Guess the correct order of these famous movie quotes from the 2000s
item.description: The ABC Timelines quiz is back to test your knowledge of some of the most iconic movie quotes of the 2000s.

item.title: Australians share the moment they realised gambling had changed their lives forever
item.description: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

item.title: The Talented Mr F follows two filmmakers trying to find their stolen movie
item.description: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

item.title: How to deal with money stress in your relationship
item.description: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

item.title: Should the tax system reward those who can afford to take risks?
item.description: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.

item.title: Young investors accept 'party had to end' but rethink financial plans after budget CGT reforms
item.description: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.

item.title: People using super for weight-loss surgery, but those looking overseas face risks
item.description: Weight-loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

item.title: The networks we rely on face growing risk from natural disasters
item.description: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

item.title: Tasmania's treasurer Eric Abetz talks a big game on debt. But how far is he willing to cut the budget?
item.description: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.

item.title: Donald Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of Gulf state leaders.

item.title: Where tonnes of Tasmania's dead salmon went
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Shallow burials, landfill: Where tonnes of Tasmania's dead salmon went
item.description: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

item.title: Granny flats allowed again in parts of Sydney's south-west near Western Sydney airport
item.description: The NSW government will permit secondary dwellings such as granny flats in some areas affected by noise from Western Sydney International Airport.

item.title: Kylie Minogue on career-spanning doco, Michael Hutchence, Nick Cave and the AFL
item.description: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

item.title: Shooters kill three people at San Diego mosque in suspected hate crime
item.description: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at San Diego's largest mosque, killing three men before they were later found dead, police say.

item.title: Large police response to San Diego mosque shooting
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pat Cummins propels Sunrisers Hyderabad into IPL playoffs after win over Chennai Super Kings
item.description: Pat Cummins delivers when his team needs it most with crucial wickets as Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings.

item.title: Stranded school students rescued from national park as deluge drenches Gold Coast
item.description: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim.

item.title: Prime minister knocks back $94 million proposal to help flooded farmers restock
item.description: Widespread flooding in north-west Queensland killed more than 90,000 head of livestock this year, but farmers will have to go it alone if they want to rebuild.

item.title: Collins-class submarine overhaul scaled back in 'pivot to reduce risk' — as it happened
item.description: Changes are being made to massive program aiming to upgrade and extend the life of Australia's aging fleet of Collins Class submarines, Defence Minister Richard Marles has confirmed.

item.title: Community in Victoria's south-east fights back against Sandy Point caravan park development proposal
item.description: A community group in Victoria's south-east will appeal a council planning decision to approve the development of a regional caravan park, which it says would be an "absolute disaster" for the region.

item.title: Bernard Tomic edges closer to shock French Open return as fellow Australians struggle in qualifiers
item.description: Bernard Tomic is just two wins from reaching his first main draw of the French Open since 2019 after being the only Australian to enjoy success in the qualifiers.

item.title: Arsenal edges closer to Premier League title after nervy win as VAR spares Kai Havertz red card
item.description: Kai Havertz emerges Arsenal's hero after scoring the winner against Burnley, and is spared a sending-off for a horror tackle.

item.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin to meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing days after Donald Trump
item.description: While this meeting comes just days after Beijing hosted the US president, the Kremlin insists there is "no correlation" between the visits.

item.title: Central Queensland ambulance drama prompts push for no-name roads investigation
item.description: When Chris Stehbens spied a lost ambulance driving up and down his road during a Triple Zero (000) emergency response, he was not surprised by the struggle to find the right address.

item.title: OJ Simpson murder trial detective Mark Fuhrman dies aged 74
item.description: Mark Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of OJ Simpson's ex-wife, Nicole Brown Simpson, and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.

item.title: Long-time Nationals MP Tim Bull decides not to contest 2026 Victorian election
item.description: Tim Bull has announced he will not contest the November election, after representing Gippsland East for the National Party for 16 years.

item.title: WHO Director-General opens 79th World Health Assembly
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ebola treatment centres to open in Congo as American doctor diagnosed
item.description: The cash-strapped World Health Organization is sending a team of experts to the Democratic Republic of the Congo as hundreds of cases of Ebola are suspected.

item.title: Northern Territory diphtheria outbreak spreads across borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia
item.description: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

item.title: Australians detained after Israel intercepted Gaza-bound flotilla
item.description: Israeli forces detain 11 Australians after its navy intercepts ships seeking to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza, flotilla organisers say.

item.title: Neymar included in Brazil's FIFA World Cup squad despite three-year international hiatus
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti includes Brazil's all-time leading goalscorer in his first FIFA World Cup squad.

item.title: Hawthorn 'extremely disappointed' after AFL forces club to end Tasmanian partnership
item.description: Hawthorn is less than pleased after being forced to cease playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the Devils' AFL entry.

item.title: Man, 47, faces court charged with murdering wife and sons in Campbelltown home
item.description: The 47-year-old has faced court charged with what police describe as the "particularly violent" murder of his wife and sons in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child in Australia
item.description: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipina heritage.

item.title: Tasmania government apologises for display of autopsy body parts in museum without consent
item.description: A formal apology is given in Tasmania's parliament for the past practice of taking human specimens from autopsies and displaying them in a museum without the knowledge of family members.

item.title: Scientists investigate mystery illness paralysing and killing hundreds of magpies
item.description: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.

item.title: Woman charged over death of 79yo man after alleged Vincentia 'road rage' assault
item.description: A woman has faced court over the death of a man she allegedly attacked in a "road rage" incident at a shopping centre car park on the NSW South Coast.

item.title: Kalman Tal paid John Yalu $5,000 cash to perform his fatal amputation, court hears
item.description: Kalman Tal, 66, died soon after he asked for his left foot to be cut off in a public park with a battery-powered saw, a court has heard, but the man who performed the amputation has pleaded not guilty to murder.

item.title: Network 10 to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses
item.description: The end of a broadcast deal between Network 10 and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.

item.title: What we know about the deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque
item.description: Two teenagers opened fire at the largest mosque in San Diego, California on Monday, killing three men. Here is what we know about the attack.

item.title: Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions gets $18.9 million in funding after sounding alarm over rising caseload
item.description: The funding will go towards 24 new, permanent positions within the office to meet its increasing case load, Attorney-General Tara Cheyne said.

item.title: Key takeaways from Sam Altman and Elon Musk's bitter legal battle over OpenAI
item.description: A legal showdown between two of the tech world's most powerful men has reached its end. Here's what we learned throughout the trial.

item.title: Brief of evidence delays for Pheobe Bishop murder-accused Tanika Bromley
item.description: The defence team for Tanika Bromley, who is charged over the murder of Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop, raises issues about delays in receiving parts of the brief of evidence.

item.title: Eligibility expanded for Queensland's free nasal spray flu vaccine program
item.description: With stocks set to expire in August, the FluMist nasal spray has been made free for all children over the age of two.

item.title: Two people die from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking NT health alert
item.description: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

item.title: Why Queensland rookie Sam Walker is the great unknown of State of Origin I
item.description: Sam Walker has always seemed like a player destined for the highest levels of rugby league, but ahead of his State of Origin debut, it's impossible to know what comes next for the new Queensland halfback.

item.title: TISM fined for $18,000 in damage to Sydney Opera House ahead of tour
item.description: The anarchic, ARIA-winning group has been hit with a hefty fine by the Sydney Opera House before its first national tour in more than 30 years.

item.title: Why Cuba is warning US military action could spark 'bloodbath' after months of rising tensions
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez says Cuba's right to defend itself should not be used by the US as justification for a potential war.

item.title: Iran crackdown drives surge in global death penalty figures, Amnesty International report says
item.description: A crackdown in Iran drove a surge in the number of people executed worldwide in 2025, but China remains the "leading executioner," human rights group Amnesty International says.

item.title: Former Brisbane GP Stan Theodoros charged with 148 counts of rape and sexual assault
item.description: A former Brisbane doctor has been charged with 148 counts of rape and sexual assault following an extensive police investigation.

item.title: Reserve Bank worries about inflation pressures building, risk of a recession
item.description: The Reserve Bank is growing increasingly anxious about price pressures building in the economy and its chief economist has raised the risk of a recession in post-budget remarks.

item.title: Rainfall 'more than double' forecast drenches SEQ, premier calls for BOM improvements
item.description: As flash flooding from record-setting rainfall recedes, some south-east Queensland residents feel that Bureau of Meteorology forecasts fell short of what was experienced.

item.title: Bureau of Meteorology executive Peter Stone, who oversaw bungled website revamp, departs role
item.description: Peter Stone was acting CEO when the BOM launched its new website, which was widely criticised for its costs and poor design.

item.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's suppression order lifts after 25 years
item.description: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.

item.title: Brisbane Heat back Cricket Australia plan to open BBL season in India
item.description: Cricket Australia has spoken with BBL franchises about a plan to open this season's BBL with a showdown in India.

item.title: Massage therapist Cameron Petrie pleads guilty to raping Sunshine Coast mother
item.description: A Queensland massage therapist has been imprisoned for raping a client who says "silence is not consent" and that the "degrading" experience devastated her emotionally, physically and financially.

item.title: Great-grandmother Jacky Hudson to represent Australia at Commonwealth Games in Glasgow
item.description: At 75 years old, Jacky Hudson will tread an unlikely path for a great-grandmother after earning selection in Australia's Commonwealth Games lawn bowls squad.

item.title: Undersea volcano erupts in Papua New Guinea's Bismarck Sea prompting tsunami concerns
item.description: The Titan Ridge Volcano is erupting in the Bismarck Sea, sending ash and smoke into the sky and prompting authorities to warn of possible tsunamis in the area.

item.title: Police investigating whether hooning complaint led to assault of Geraldton father
item.description: Geraldton police investigate whether complaints about hooning were behind an alleged assault that left a father-of-three in a critical condition.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Peter Hollingworth, former governor-general of Australia and retired Anglican bishop, dies aged 91
item.description: Peter Hollingworth was the first and only cleric to serve as governor-general, before his resignation in 2003 amid his handling of historical church abuse scandals.

item.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's prescription medication
item.description: An 82-year-old South Australian man died after he ingested prescription medication that was meant for another person and had been incorrectly labelled, a coronial inquest is told.

item.title: Musk v Altman – the clash of the tech titans
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Grattan Institute report calls for abolition of parking minimum requirements across Australia
item.description: Australia is wasting more than $1 billion each year building off-street car parks that end up unused and unwanted, a Grattan report has found.

item.title: Far-right Israeli minister accuses ICC of making 'secret' request to arrest him
item.description: Reports have been circulating that the ICC is preparing to issue warrants against Israel's far-right finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich.

item.title: Vladimir Putin lands in China for summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping days after Donald Trump's visit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin smiles as he greets Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi at the airport in Beijing.

item.title: Travel quiz, May 20: Got the Wednesday blues? Let's take a trip around the world
item.description: Can you name the city in this picture? And what is the Canadian specialty food "beaver tail"?

item.title: Angine de Poitrine, the French Canadian costumed microtonal duo breaking the internet
item.description: French Canadian duo Angine de Poitrine are riding a wave of interest after a video of their live performance exploded in popularity, aided by a comical reaction by many on YouTube struggling to understand how their music is made.

item.title: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors
item.description: Labor MPs are gearing up for a hard sell on the impact of the federal government's tax changes on some small businesses, trusts and shares, despite many reporting "positive" feedback on housing reforms.

item.title: Life on the islands of the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran war
item.description: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.

item.title: Vanuatu agrees to pacts with China and Australia
item.description: Jotham Napat has confirmed Vanuatu has agreed to two pacts with both countries, but is not happy with either party.

item.title: Small businesses and startups go viral with AI Albo but tax experts 'rubbish' CGT claims
item.description: One startup founder who helped spread viral memes about capital gains tax reforms says the campaign was aimed at attracting attention after the budget, and not necessarily focused on accuracy.

item.title: Contentious developments receiving fast-tracked approval under affordable housing pathway
item.description: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.

item.title: Can alternative content help to fight the influence of the 'manosphere'?
item.description: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

item.title: Formal complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into The Hobart Clinic's corporate governance
item.description: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.

item.title: After booting Hawthorn out of Tasmania, the Devils must deliver for Launceston
item.description: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.

item.title: UK police investigating child sex abuse allegations relating to Epstein files
item.description: A British police force has said it is investigating two allegations of child sex abuse following information in documents relating to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Young women behind growing interest in high-octane motorsport
item.description: A former track photographer, Georgia Tillyer bought her car, lovingly-named Night Fury, and learnt how to drive manual to enter her first event at Sydney Motorsport Park.

item.title: Police union alleges 'defunct' corrections work practices behind record watch house overcrowding
item.description: A man died while in the custody of Queensland's most crowded police watch house. The police union says the bottleneck is being caused by outdated prison service work practices.

item.title: Arsenal wins Premier League after 22-year drought following Manchester City's draw against Bournemouth
item.description: Arsenal's long wait for a Premier League title comes to an end as title challengers Manchester City drop points at Bournemouth.

item.title: Former senior NT child protection staffers speak out amid sector's condemnation of legislative changes
item.description: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

item.title: Will double-strength bait stop Western and South Australia's mouse plague?
item.description: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

item.title: Chalmers returns serve to NSW premier over tax comments — as it happened
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has rejected NSW Premier Chris Minns's comments on bracket creep.

item.title: How crepuscular rays are creating 'beautiful' sunsets across the Top End
item.description: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.

item.title: FBI says San Diego mosque shooters met online and left writings expressing hate
item.description: Authorities have revealed the teenagers who killed three people at a San Diego mosque met online and shared a "broad hatred" toward different religions and races.

item.title: Southampton expelled from Premier League playoffs for spying on rivals
item.description: Southampton loses its chance to gain promotion to the Premier League after being caught spying on Middlesbrough and two other rivals.

item.title: One killed, four injured in 'brazen, violent attack' inside Sydney home
item.description: A man with links to rap group OneFour has died and four others have been seriously injured following a Canley Heights shooting, which is believed to be organised crime-related.

item.title: Trump allies can seek payouts from $2.5b fund for 'lawfare' victims
item.description: The US has set up a fund to compensate Americans deemed victims of "lawfare". Democrats say it is "pure theft of public funds" to pay the president's allies.

item.title: More ISIS-linked women and children set to return to Australia
item.description: Six Australian women, along with their children and grandchildren, look likely to leave a camp in Syria within days.

item.title: Earthquake in outback SA has 'permanently changed landscape'
item.description: Cracks in the ground in outback Australia caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake could help researchers predict future seismic activity.

item.title: ASX falls below 8,500 level as rate rise fears surface — as it happened
item.description: The Australian share market has fallen below 8,500 points after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it.

item.title: Fisheries body pushes for carp virus trial in Victoria's Wimmera as frustrations grow over delays
item.description: The Victorian Fisheries Authority wants to host a carp virus trial, but the federal government says it needs regulatory approval, which will delay research.

item.title: Arsenal celebrate winning first Premier League title in 22 years
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Researchers uncover longest whale journey ever documented with fluke photos
item.description: Scientists analysing a database of photos find two humpback whales travelled from Queensland to Brazil, covering a record distance of 14,200km.

item.title: Man charged with Arnhem Land murder bailed to live with family 500km away in Tiwi Islands
item.description: A 20-year-old man charged with the murder of another man during a brawl in Arnhem Land last month has been bailed to live with family far from the location of the death, in the Tiwi Islands.

item.title: Kylie Minogue on her new three-part docuseries KYLIE
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Storm Hunter and Tristan Schoolkate advance in French Open qualifiers as seeded stars fall
item.description: Storm Hunter is a step closer to her first French Open main draw appearance since 2023, as Priscilla Hon and Maddison Inglis fall short in the qualifiers.

item.title: NRL confirms Tigers, Eels, Titans and Storm will play 2027 Las Vegas fixtures
item.description: Jarome Luai will begin his final season with the Wests Tigers in Las Vegas, with the NRL unveiling its teams for next year's season-openers.

item.title: Israel says no live ammunition used by troops filmed firing at Gaza flotilla boats
item.description: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

item.title: Melbourne's Bianca Adler, 18, becomes youngest Australian to summit Mount Everest
item.description: A social media post just before 8am AEST announced that Bianca Adler had reached the top of the world's tallest mountain.

item.title: Homesharer allegedly accessed disabled man's bank account to spend over $54,000
item.description: A 70-year-old appointed homesharer faces court charged with 264 fraud offences after allegedly accessing the bank account of an intellectually disabled man who was living with him and spending over $54,000.

item.title: Maldives investigates if Italian divers went too deep in fatal cave dive
item.description: A Maldives government official says a permit was given to study soft corals, but authorities were unaware the group planned to cave dive.

item.title: Plans to revive WA's once-booming nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices
item.description: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

item.title: AVG Travels fallout deepens as more customers report cancelled or rescheduled tours
item.description: The ABC has been contacted by more than 100 travellers since first reporting on the matter on Monday, with some claiming their tours were cancelled at the last minute and itineraries changed.

item.title: Live: Xi and Putin deliver joint statement to media after Kremlin sets 'very serious expectations'
item.description: The two leaders are expected to discuss bilateral economic relations and "key international and regional issues", according to the Kremlin. Follow live.

item.title: Greenland PM repeats message to US that the nation is not for sale after Trump envoy talks
item.description: Jeff Landry, appointed by Donald Trump in December as the US special envoy to Greenland, has met the country's prime minister in the capital Nuuk.

item.title: What life is like as an older parent, or being born to one
item.description: After publishing our story on having babies in your 40s, we heard from readers on what being, or having, an older parent is like.

item.title: James Blee, 24, found guilty of helping father with Newcastle cocaine shipment
item.description: A Queensland man has been found guilty of involvement with his father's attempt to smuggle a massive amount of cocaine into the NSW port city.

item.title: NSW MP Mark Latham's former partner Nathalie Matthews withdraws application for private AVO
item.description: A private apprehended violence order (AVO) application against NSW independent MP Mark Latham that was initiated by his former partner has been withdrawn.

item.title: Fraud case dropped against Australian Olympic swimmer Neil Brooks
item.description: Neil Brooks and his wife Linda Brooks were both charged with one count of fraud in 2017, but were formally discharged on Wednesday.

item.title: Matildas squad named for Mexico friendlies with teen keeper included
item.description: Mary Fowler, Ellie Carpenter and Steph Catley are back in the Matildas squad for games against Mexico, while a teenager has been called up.

item.title: Putin receives red carpet welcome upon arrival in China
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Apprehension as Dhilmar buys one of Australia's largest coal mining operations
item.description: Anglo American has operated metallurgical coal mines in Queensland since the turn of the century. Now, a UK-based company has bought them all.

item.title: Federal government prepares diphtheria support package after more than 220 cases reported
item.description: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

item.title: Tasmanian government businesses defy premier's call for public disclosure logs
item.description: Tasmanian government-owned businesses or companies are being threatened with legislation to force them to disclose right-to-information documents publicly after nine of them failed to respond to the premier's request.

item.title: Ocean Census discovers over 1,100 new marine species
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Marine species encountered on Ocean Census expeditions
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ocean Census project discovers 1,121 new species of ocean life around the world in one year
item.description: Thousands of species have been discovered lurking and thriving in our oceans, as part of an effort to catalogue 100,000 new species.

item.title: San Diego mosque shooting victims hailed as heroes and mourned by community
item.description: Three people killed by two shooters at a San Diego mosque were beloved pillars of the community and died while saving 140 children in the building at the time of the attack, authorities say.

item.title: Three Aussies in Giro d'Italia top 10 after key time trial as Filippo Ganna claims victory
item.description: Ben O'Connor uses his Perth experience to impress in the Giro d'Italia's only time trial stage of 2026, leapfrogging fellow West Australian Jai Hindley in the overall standings, with another Perth boy Michael Storer just behind them.

item.title: Xi Jinping welcomes Vladimir Putin to Beijing's Great Hall
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Doctor fined $30k for self-publishing unauthorised pictures of patients
item.description: An anaesthetist who worked at Toowoomba Hospital has been fined $30,000 for professional misconduct after he included confidential patient information in a self-published book.

item.title: Jai Arrow, South Sydney and Queensland Maroons star, retires from NRL due to motor neurone disease
item.description: Rabbitohs and Maroons enforcer Jai Arrow announces he will step away from rugby league after being diagnosed with motor neurone disease.

item.title: Married at First Sight UK pulled
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NT Police officer found not guilty of coercing former partner to withdraw assault charge
item.description: A Northern Territory Police officer has been cleared of coercing his former partner into withdrawing an assault charge after facing trial on one count of attempting to pervert the course of justice.

item.title: Man jailed over manslaughter of surfer Guy Haymes in Manly apartment
item.description: Guy Haymes died after being assaulted in a dispute over $50 at a Manly apartment in February 2024.

item.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rebounds above $4,000 per tonne
item.description: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.

item.title: Visy recycling plant still leaves smell over Reservoir after years of complaints
item.description: A urban planning expert says Victoria's industrial zoning laws are outdated and that operations like Visy's paper recycling plant in Reservoir may no longer be appropriate so close to housing.

item.title: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia and so is misinformation about the disease
item.description: Falsehoods about diphtheria are spreading online as a worsening outbreak of the disease affects Indigenous communities.

item.title: Queensland hit by triple injury blow for State of Origin III as Blues look for rare series whitewash
item.description: Injuries to three key players have compounded Queensland's task to avoid a 3-0 women's State of Origin whitewash.

item.title: Teenagers arrested for alleged arson attacks on Melbourne nightclubs
item.description: Victoria Police allege the three males are responsible for arson attacks at nightclubs La Di Da and Electric Bar earlier this month.

item.title: Trial hears Kalman Tal offered cash for amputation before his death
item.description: Witnesses have given evidence about a 66-year-old Queensland man shopping around for someone in his town to amputate his leg in the weeks before he died at a public park.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.

item.title: NSW Auditor-General finds flaws in Australia's costliest disaster recovery program
item.description: A new report finds a disaster recovery program set up after the devastating 2022 floods in northern NSW is yet to deliver any of the homes it promised.

item.title: Death of former governor-general Peter Hollingworth sparks difficult feelings for church child abuse victim
item.description: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth, who resigned over his handling of child abuse in the church, died in Melbourne on Tuesday at the age of 91.

item.title: Carlos Alcaraz rules himself out of Wimbledon, entire grass-court swing, with wrist injury
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.

item.title: Health officials warn diphtheria cases could rise in biggest outbreak on record
item.description: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

item.title: Canberra woman wins legal fight against Hellenic Club over fall on cracked tile
item.description: The woman, who was awarded $285,000, rated her pain as 10 out of 10 and spent three nights in hospital.

item.title: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy to be released on home detention
item.description: Paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy will be released from jail when his sentence expires next month, despite an ongoing application to have him indefinitely detained.

item.title: Adelaide Hills man killed mother with pickaxe handle while under 'supernatural' attack, court told
item.description: An Adelaide Hills man standing trial for the murder of his mother describes to the Supreme Court how he repeatedly struck her while he was panicked and "under attack" by "supernatural beings". Warning: This story contains images and details that may be distressing to some readers.

item.title: AI firm Anthropic's 'dangerous' Mythos model fuelling government's desire to lure company to Australia
item.description: A new generation of AI models deemed "too dangerous" for public release is driving Australia's efforts to lure top AI companies to establish a major presence in the country.

item.title: Thailand cuts visa-free stay period for more than 90 countries, citing crimes by foreigners
item.description: Thailand is reducing the visa-free period available to visitors from 90 countries, including Australia, with the duration decided on a country-by-country basis.

item.title: WA prosecutor says 'man shouldn't have been driving' when alleged hit and run occurred, killing a Katanning man
item.description: The 26-year-old allegedly struck the victim with his Toyota Hilux as he was leaving for work, causing 'catastrophic' head injuries.

item.title: John Howard and Everald Compton speaking at a press conference about the Inland Rail
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The Inland Rail is paused but the father of it has a plan to see it through to completion by his 100th birthday
item.description: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.

item.title: Lawyers say teen who allegedly tried to hijack plane at Avalon Airport was mentally impaired
item.description: A teen who allegedly tried to hijack a plane at Avalon Airport felt relieved when passengers restrained him, describing it as a "weight off his shoulders", his lawyers say.

item.title: San Diego white supremacist shooters inspired by Australia’s worst mass murderer
item.description: Thousands of kilometres away, but the terrible connection to Australia is evident. ABC NEWS Verify looks at the inspiration behind the San Diego Islamic Centre murders.

item.title: Queensland's new public hospital to refuse voluntary assisted dying care, abortions and contraception for religious reasons
item.description: The Queensland premier has opened the state's first major new public hospital in almost a decade — at Springfield, south-west of Brisbane — but it won't offer some services for religious reasons.

item.title: Third attempt to change SA's abortion laws since 2024 go before state parliament
item.description: South Australia's parliament is set to debate restricting abortion access for the third time in less than two years, with One Nation's breakthrough election result meaning the laws could pass the upper house.

item.title: Brothers of Bondi hero Ahmed Al Ahmed front court over alleged threatening phone call
item.description: Two brothers of Ahmed Al Ahmed faced Bankstown Local Court over alleged verbal threats via the phone.

item.title: Train crashes into truck at Perth level crossing in Guildford
item.description: Emergency services are at the scene of an incident in the eastern Perth suburb of Guildford, where a train is believed to have struck a truck at a level crossing.

item.title: Vladimir Putin meets 'old friend' Xi Jinping in Beijing and secures more than 20 agreements
item.description: The pair agreed to a raft of deals in front of invited press less than a week after Donald Trump left China's capital with no confirmed deals.
